parts.title: The Shore
parts.text: Aanya walked along the beach with her **crew** of two best friends, Kiran and Arnav, ready to explore the tide pools. She knelt down to **trace** the outline of a starfish in her notebook, carefully following every arm. Rocky cliffs **surround** the cove on all sides, giving the spot a feeling of calm and mystery.

parts.title: Nature's Warning
parts.text: It was **obvious** to all three of them that the sky was changing — dark clouds gathered faster than they expected. Arnav, the most **clever** of the group, used a tide chart to track the **origin** of the incoming storm. A **cascade** of rainwater began pouring down the cliff face in thin silver streams. The tide was rising fast and would soon cut off the path, so they moved quickly toward the shore.

parts.title: The Rescue
parts.text: That's when Aanya spotted something on the **surface** of the water — a baby sea turtle tangled in an old fishing net. The net seemed to **contain** a ball of kelp and rope all knotted together. Kiran, who was training as a marine biologist, said the turtle belonged to a rare species found in this part of the sea. The net was knotted like the work of an old **weaver**, but Arnav carefully cut it free. "This little one will **attract** a crowd at the research center," Kiran said with a grin.

parts.title: New Purpose
parts.text: The baby turtle swam away into the waves, strong and free. Back on shore, the three friends were quiet for a moment, watching it disappear into the deep blue. Kiran invited Aanya to join her summer marine research program. Arnav said he wanted to enter a science **tournament** with their findings about the tide pools. From that day on, all three of them knew the ocean was not just a place to visit — it was a world worth protecting.

definitions.meaning1: A competition with many games or matches to decide a winner. Example: Our school chess club entered a tournament and competed against teams from five other schools.
definitions.studentChallenge: The soccer ___ lasted all weekend, and our team made it to the final round.

definitions.meaning1: To draw over the lines of something or follow a path. Example: The student used thin paper to trace the outline of the map for her geography project.
definitions.meaning2: A very small amount of something left behind. Example: The detective found a trace of mud on the floor that was an important clue.
definitions.studentChallenge: She used tracing paper to ___ the picture of the butterfly and then colored it in.

definitions.meaning1: To be all around something or someone on every side. Example: Tall pine trees surround the lake, making it a beautiful and quiet place to visit.
definitions.studentChallenge: A moat filled with water used to ___ the castle and protect it from enemies.

definitions.meaning1: A group of people who work together, especially on a ship, airplane, or project. Example: The ship's crew worked together to steer the vessel safely through the rough storm.
definitions.studentChallenge: The film ___ set up cameras and lights before the actors arrived on set.

definitions.meaning1: Quick to learn and understand; smart. Example: The clever fox outsmarted the hunters.
definitions.studentChallenge: That was a ___ way to solve the puzzle.

definitions.meaning1: To pull toward or cause interest. Example: The bright flowers attract butterflies.
definitions.studentChallenge: The magnet will ___ the metal clips.

definitions.meaning1: A small waterfall, or a series of things falling or flowing downward. Example: A beautiful cascade of water tumbled over the rocks and into the pool below.
definitions.studentChallenge: After the rainstorm, a ___ of water poured down the hillside and into the creek.

definitions.meaning1: Easy to see or understand; clear. Example: It was obvious that she was tired.
definitions.studentChallenge: The answer was ___ once she read the question again.

definitions.meaning1: The beginning or starting point of something; where something comes from. Example: The origin of the river is a tiny spring high up in the mountains.
definitions.studentChallenge: The teacher asked us to research the ___ of our favourite holiday traditions.

definitions.meaning1: To hold something inside. Example: The big treasure chest was said to contain gold coins and sparkling jewels.
definitions.studentChallenge: This jar can ___ up to one hundred marbles if you pack them tightly.

definitions.meaning1: A person who makes cloth or fabric by crossing threads over and under each other. Example: The skilled weaver sat at her loom and created a beautiful blanket with colorful patterns.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ used bright red and blue threads to create a stunning tapestry.

definitions.meaning1: The outside or top layer of something. Example: The surface of the frozen pond was smooth enough for ice skating.
definitions.meaning2: To come up to the top from below. Example: The whale would surface to breathe and then dive back down into the deep ocean.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ of the table was covered with scratches from years of use.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Our school chess club entered a ___ and competed against teams from five other schools.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Tall pine trees ___ the lake, making it a beautiful and quiet place to visit.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: A moat filled with water used to ___ the castle and protect it from enemies.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ship's ___ worked together to steer the vessel safely through the rough storm.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The film ___ set up cameras and lights before the actors arrived on set.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ fox outsmarted the hunters by hiding its tracks in the stream.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: That was a ___ way to solve the puzzle — no one else had thought of it.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The bright flowers ___ butterflies from all over the garden.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The magnet will ___ the metal clips from across the table.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: A beautiful ___ of water tumbled over the rocks and into the pool below.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: After the rainstorm, a ___ of water poured down the hillside and into the creek.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: It was ___ that she was tired — she could barely keep her eyes open.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The answer was ___ once she read the question again carefully.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ of the river is a tiny spring high up in the mountains.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The teacher asked us to research the ___ of our favourite holiday traditions.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The big treasure chest was said to ___ gold coins and sparkling jewels.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: This jar can ___ up to one hundred marbles if you pack them tightly.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The skilled ___ sat at her loom and created a beautiful blanket with colorful patterns.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The ___ used bright red and blue threads to create a stunning tapestry.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The ___ of the frozen pond was smooth enough for ice skating.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The whale came up to the ___ of the ocean to breathe before diving back down.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage: Arnav and his sailing _(1)_ — a group of twelve friends who worked together on the boat — set off to explore a hidden cove. It was _(2)_ to everyone, plain and clear, that the weather was perfect. Arnav used a map to _(3)_ the route his grandfather had once traveled, following every twist and turn. Tall cliffs _(4)_ the cove on all sides, wrapping around it like walls. The _(5)_ of the calm water was so glassy it reflected the sky like a mirror. A _(6)_ of sparkling water tumbled down the cliff face, flowing from rock to rock into the sea below. The bright colors of the coral were sure to _(7)_ curious fish from the deeper water. They found a cave that seemed to _(8)_ hidden treasures, its walls packed with glittering stones. Inside, a local _(9)_ had left a woven tapestry hanging on the wall, each thread crossing beautifully under and over. The inscription described a great sailing _(10)_ — a competition held there long ago, with many boats racing for the championship. It also told the _(11)_ of the cove — where it began and how it got its name. Arnav was _(12)_, smart enough to photograph everything before the tide came in.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
